root_motivation: There is no canonical record of which watchman roots should be watched; reboots silently drop the live set, leaving wchg returning empty with no diagnostic. A versioned declared-roots manifest and a pure reconcile function are the prerequisite for any tool that can restore, probe, or validate the watch set automatically.
user_persona: Joe Yen, running wintermute tooling on an Arch Linux laptop, using wchg/recall daily. Wchg silently fails after every reboot until roots are manually re-watched. This is the foundational crate for the anchor fleet of tools.

acceptance_criteria.description: cargo build and cargo test succeed offline; a test asserts reconcile makes zero backend calls (it operates only on the two passed slices + the injected now).
RootStatus, WatchRoot, WatchState, ReconcileAction, ReconcilePlan are public and serde-(de)serializable; a round-trip test covers each.
RootsConfig::load parses config/roots.example.toml; a test with a fixture manifest yields the expected Vec<WatchRoot>, including a per-root max_age_secs override.

reconcile classifies correctly against a FakeBackend snapshot: declared absent from live => Missing+Watch; watched with clock older than max_age_secs => Stale+ReseedCursor; live not in manifest => Undeclared+NoteUndeclared; fresh watched => Watched+NoOp.
anchor plan --format json emits one entry per declared root plus undeclared-live notes; the schema matches the documented ReconcilePlan.
anchor plan exits non-zero when at least one declared root is Missing, zero when all are Watched/Stale only; covered by two integration cases driving a FakeBackend.
anchor plan | head -1 does not panic (SIGPIPE reset verified by a test that closes the read end early).
README documents the manifest format, the type surface, and the WatchBackend trait so anchor-probe / anchor-reconcile / anchor-boot have a contract to extend.

scope: Declare a TOML manifest of watch roots (~/.config/anchor/roots.toml)
RootsConfig::load parses the manifest into Vec<WatchRoot>
Core types: WatchRoot, WatchState, RootStatus, ReconcileAction, ReconcilePlan — all public and serde-serializable
Pure reconcile(declared, live, now) -> ReconcilePlan with no side effects
anchor plan subcommand: table output + --format json; exits nonzero on Missing roots
SIGPIPE reset in main() so anchor plan | head never panics
config/roots.example.toml shipping the Joe Yen daily roots
non_goals: No --apply: anchor-reconcile (a later PRD) owns the apply step
No daemon / watch loop: anchor is a one-shot CLI per invocation
No auto-creation of roots.toml: the user owns their manifest
No removal of Undeclared roots: the tool is informational only for live-but-undeclared roots
No cross-machine fleet sync: that is the constellation PRD family

additional.sigpipe_reset: sigpipe::reset() must be first line of main()

five_whys_trace.q: Why do we need a declared-roots manifest?
five_whys_trace.a: Because watchman drops watched roots on reboot, and there is no record of which roots should be watched.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does losing the roots matter?
five_whys_trace.a: Because wchg delta queries return silently empty or error without saying why, making the tool unreliable after every reboot.

five_whys_trace.q: Why can't we just re-watch roots in the self-review script?
five_whys_trace.a: We do — but the set being re-watched lives only in the script, not in a versioned manifest. Any change to the set requires editing scripts in multiple places.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does the set need to be versioned?
five_whys_trace.a: So the reconcile tool can diff declared-vs-live and emit a deterministic, testable plan rather than a procedural script that diverges from reality.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should anchor plan call the real watchman binary or always use FakeBackend?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Shipped with a real WatchmanBackend that shells watchman watch-list; tests use FakeBackend injected via the WatchBackend trait.

ambiguities_resolved.question: What is the unfakeable metric when the primary value is correctness of reconcile logic?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: acceptance_tests_passing_count — 8 ACs, all MUST. Build does not pass unless all 8 pass.
